title: ranunculus propinquus subsp. glabriusculus
intro: Ranunculus propinquus subsp. glabriusculus, also known as Crowfoot, is a rare, aquatic plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Crowfoot include its ability to relieve joint pain and swelling, as well as its potential to reduce the risk of certain cancers. In herbal preparations, Crowfoot is often used as a tincture or infused in hot water as a tea, and can also be applied topically to the affected area as a poultice.
